# xvn — Operator documentation

> Non-custodial AI trading agents that improve themselves.
> Source-of-truth for embedded agents and reviewers.

## Audience

This site is for operators running an xvn node, hackathon judges, and AI
agents reading the repository. Every HTML page has a Markdown mirror with
the same basename ending in `.md`.

## Canonical pages

- [Overview](index.md): What xvn does, what it does not, quickstart, safety model.
- [Operator manual](manual.md): Tiered milestones, paper trading, Orderly onboarding, ERC-8004 mint, dashboard auth, observability.
- [CLI reference](cli.md): 16 verbs. Argv-only contract. Strategy authoring, eval runs, A/B compare, providers, remote CLI over Tailscale.
- [Architecture](architecture.md): Four-stage Intern → Trader → Risk → Execution pipeline; deterministic risk layer; eval framework with pre-committed Δ-Sharpe; ERC-8004 identity (opt-in).

## Optional context

- [Sitemap](sitemap.json): Machine-readable index — title, summary, key terms, mirror paths.
- Source repo: https://github.com/latentwill/xvision
- Commit: a73b18f
- Updated: 2026-05-20

## Hard rules for agents acting on this codebase

1. Never run `cargo` on server/deploy hosts.
2. Never do production image builds on server/deploy hosts.
3. Build deploy images on a build/control host, then ship or pull the runtime image.
4. Do not assume arbitrary SSH access to live nodes — use the Tailscale-served dashboard's typed remote-CLI job API or `scripts/xvn-remote.py`.
5. `xvision-identity` is opt-in. Keep it excluded from default workspace builds; compile explicitly with `cargo build -p xvision-identity` only when wiring identity.
6. `xvn dashboard`, `xvn mcp`, and `xvn fire-trade` are rejected by the remote-CLI job API even in devmode.
7. Non-loopback dashboard binds require `XVN_DASHBOARD_TOKEN`; the process refuses to start without it.
8. Treat the Stage 1 Intern as an evidence preparer, not a recommender. Do not add `candidate_direction` or `candidate_size_bps` to its schema.

## Entry points for embedded coding agents

1. `MANUAL.md` — operator commands and environment assumptions.
2. `FOLLOWUPS.md` — active engineering tracks and deferred work.
3. `.claude/skills/xvision-cli/SKILL.md` — operator and usage tasks.
4. `.claude/skills/xvision-dev/SKILL.md` — editing the codebase.
5. `xvn --help` — typed CLI surface; argv-only, no shell text.
